coverage_profile.firm: Mizuho (most recent observed in calls 2024-Q1 through 2025-Q3)
coverage_profile.seniority_signal: Title moves from Senior Analyst (Credit Suisse, 2022) to Managing Director / Senior Equity Analyst at Mizuho by 2024-Q4/2025-Q1; management routinely answers his follow-ups, indicating recognized senior coverage and repeat access. (See 2022-Q1 as Credit Suisse; 2024-Q4/2025-Q1 listed as Managing Director / Mizuho.)
coverage_profile.sector_lens: Cruise / leisure / travel operator-focused: heavy emphasis on private destinations (CocoCay/Perfect Day, Paradise Island/Nassau, Costa Maya), onboard/ancillary economics, yields/APCD and ship deployments; occasionally extends into river/cruise capital allocation. (See repeated destination questions across 2022-Q4 → 2025-Q2 and river questions in 2025-Q3.)
rhetorical_signature: opens with "Hey, how's it going?" in 6 of 12 calls (e.g., 2022-Q1, 2022-Q2, 2022-Q3, 2022-Q4, 2023-Q1, 2025-Q1).
prefers tight, conversational phrasing and quick two-part questions (examples: 2022-Q4: "Is this simply occupancy improving or is the revenue per Passenger Cruise Day also expected to improve?").
often closes segments with a short acknowledgement ('Gotcha.' / 'Thanks.') and routinely asks immediate follow-ups on numeric details.

reasoning_style.follow_up_pattern: Multi-part / sequential questioner who frequently takes a second turn for clarification or deeper drill. ≈ 18 of 39 turns (≈46%) contain ≥2 sub-questions or follow-ups (examples: 2022-Q2 cash-flow + working-capital; 2024-Q4 Nassau pricing + net-cruise-cost).
reasoning_style.evidence_demanded: He consistently pushes for specific numbers, guidance and mechanisms — dollars, passenger counts, and attach rates (requests for $500 million operating cash flow in 2022-Q2; "3,000 a day" Hideaway in 2022-Q4; "2.5 million–3 million guests" for CocoCay in 2023-Q1). Count: ≈12 turns request explicit numeric detail.
reasoning_style.anchoring_habits: He anchors to (1) management's most-recent on-call statements (e.g., cites "$500 million" operating cash flow — 2022-Q2; "140 basis points" yield wording — 2025-Q1), (2) the 2019 baseline or pre-COVID comps when probing mix/yield (see 2022-Q1/2022-Q4), and (3) the company’s public guidance/ship timing when reconciling APCD impact (2025-Q1). He rarely anchors to peers.

core_topics.what_they_press_on: Economics and capacity of Royal's private destinations (CocoCay, Hideaway, Paradise/Nassau, Costa Maya): pricing premiums, incremental guest spend, and whether new sites are accretive to margin/EBITDA. He repeatedly asks about capacity numbers and ramp (e.g., "2.5 million–3 million guests", "3,000 a day").
Day-pass pricing, ticketed beach-club economics vs Perfect Day free/ancillary model, and attach/adoption rates (asks for expected attach %, dynamic pricing approach and modeling inputs).
How management plans to message protocol changes or destination launches to customers and distribution (asks about CDC/protocol messaging and marketing differentiation between destinations).
Why newly delivered ships can be a near-term headline yield/headwind (timing, lower APCDs, ramp and test cruises) and the magnitude (referencing mgmt's "140 basis points" comment).
How private-destination operating cost structures (NCC) compare to CocoCay scale; whether costs for Nassau/Costa Maya are built into the company NCC assumptions and the implication for 2025 modeling.
Opportunity in under‑penetrated domestic catchments (Texas/Gulf Coast): whether lack of destinations is the key constraint and how new beach clubs unlock drivable / short-product demand.
How the company will allocate capital to high-ROI adjacent businesses (river ships / Celebrity expansion): options limit, balance-sheet considerations and sequencing of orders.

recurring_concerns.blind_spots: No evidence in this history of questions on fuel hedging or fuel-hedge policy (n_turns = 0) and effectively no questions on loyalty program metrics (enterprise loyalty / reciprocity) despite management raising loyalty in presentations. Peers sometimes press these; Ben does not in the provided record.
recurring_concerns.stance_drift: Early in the sample (2022-Q1 → 2022-Q2) he focused on onboard spend, pre-cruise commerce and near-term cash flow/working capital (see 2022-Q1 and 2022-Q2). From 2022-Q4 → 2025-Q2 his emphasis shifted heavily toward private-destination economics, pricing/attach and capacity (CocoCay → Paradise/Nassau → Costa Maya), while later (2025-Q3) he added river/cruise capital allocation to his regular destination/economics focus.

queue_behavior.typical_position: #3–#8 of ≈8 analysts; was #2 on 2 of 12 calls (2024-Q1 and 2025-Q1).
queue_behavior.default_first_pick: When early (#2–#3) he typically opens on destination/demand or ancillary economics (examples: 2024-Q1 asked Paradise Island positioning; 2025-Q1 opened on Nassau pricing; 2022-Q1 early asked about onboard spend).

adaptation_when_favorites_taken.reframe_deeper: Yes — he re-asks topics at a deeper modeling angle (e.g., 2025-Q1: asked why new ships are a headwind and invoked "140 basis points" to probe mechanics). Quote: "140 basis points" (2025-Q1).
adaptation_when_favorites_taken.white_space: Yes — he pivots to working-capital / cash-flow mechanics or regional penetration when headline topics were discussed earlier (2022-Q2: "Is there anything on the working capital side I need to consider").
adaptation_when_favorites_taken.reactive_followup: Yes — he regularly piggybacks on a specific phrase or mgmt number just delivered (e.g., 2025-Q2: asked about Jason's "same size as Magic Kingdom" remark). Quote: "same size as Magic Kingdom" (2025-Q2).

explicit_peer_references.examples: no evidence in history of explicit "following up on X's question" phrasing in Ben's questions

cross_analyst_reactivity.operator_or_followup_signal: He frequently takes a second turn within the same call for clarification or deeper drill rather than a new topic (examples: multi-question sequence on CocoCay/Hideaway in 2022-Q4 and follow-up clarification on Nassau pricing across 2024-Q4 → 2025-Q1).
